annotations.text: The most defensible primary diagnosis in this patient is **drug‑induced toxic epidermal necrolysis**, most likely triggered by vancomycin, with a possible contributory role of methotrexate.
Cytotoxic T‑cell–mediated keratinocyte apoptosis, driven by granulysin, perforin‑granzyme pathways, and Fas‑FasL interactions, culminates in full‑thickness epidermal necrosis.
The reaction is typically precipitated by an offending drug that acts as a hapten or triggers a drug‑specific T‑cell response.
the SCORTEN score, which incorporates age >40 y, malignancy, >10 % BSA detachment, serum urea >10 mmol/L, glucose >14 mmol/L, and bicarbonate <20 mmol/L.
uption to full‑thickness epidermal detachment, mucosal involvement, and confirmatory biopsy unequivocally establishes TEN as the underlying dermatologic diagnosis.
